# The Sopranos Wiki

> An open-source reference and critical analysis of The Sopranos — David Chase's HBO series, all 86 episodes, the characters, the cast and writers' room, the 2021 prequel film, and the ending nobody has stopped arguing about.

All content is licensed CC BY-SA 4.0 and free to cite. This file is a curated map for language models and answer engines — see https://llmstxt.org. Independent fan project; not affiliated with or endorsed by HBO or Warner Bros. Discovery.

## Start here
- [Start Here](https://joedef.github.io/sopranos-wiki/start-here/): where to begin, is season one slow, the four episodes that decide it
- [The Ending](https://joedef.github.io/sopranos-wiki/the-ending/): what happens at Holsten's, the cut to black, and every statement David Chase has made since 2007

## The series
- [Series overview](https://joedef.github.io/sopranos-wiki/series/): all six seasons compared
- [Season 1](https://joedef.github.io/sopranos-wiki/series/season-1/) through [Season 6](https://joedef.github.io/sopranos-wiki/series/season-6/): full episode tables with writers, directors, air dates
- [The Many Saints of Newark](https://joedef.github.io/sopranos-wiki/series/the-many-saints-of-newark/): the 2021 prequel film

## Characters
- [Characters index](https://joedef.github.io/sopranos-wiki/characters/) and [family tree](https://joedef.github.io/sopranos-wiki/characters/family-tree/)
- [Tony Soprano](https://joedef.github.io/sopranos-wiki/characters/tony-soprano/): DiMeo boss and therapy patient; the arc and the performance
- [Carmela](https://joedef.github.io/sopranos-wiki/characters/carmela-soprano/), [Dr. Melfi](https://joedef.github.io/sopranos-wiki/characters/dr-melfi/), [Christopher](https://joedef.github.io/sopranos-wiki/characters/christopher-moltisanti/), [Livia](https://joedef.github.io/sopranos-wiki/characters/livia-soprano/), [Adriana](https://joedef.github.io/sopranos-wiki/characters/adriana-la-cerva/)

## People
- [People index](https://joedef.github.io/sopranos-wiki/people/): cast, creators, crew, and the Emmy ledger
- [David Chase](https://joedef.github.io/sopranos-wiki/people/david-chase/), [James Gandolfini](https://joedef.github.io/sopranos-wiki/people/james-gandolfini/), [Edie Falco](https://joedef.github.io/sopranos-wiki/people/edie-falco/)

## Analysis
- [Analysis index](https://joedef.github.io/sopranos-wiki/analysis/): original critical essays
- [Reading the Ending](https://joedef.github.io/sopranos-wiki/analysis/reading-the-ending/): four interpretations of the cut to black, weighed, no verdict
- [Themes](https://joedef.github.io/sopranos-wiki/analysis/themes/), [Tony's Arc](https://joedef.github.io/sopranos-wiki/analysis/tonys-arc/), [Therapy & Melfi](https://joedef.github.io/sopranos-wiki/analysis/therapy-and-melfi/)

## Data & reference
- [Timeline](https://joedef.github.io/sopranos-wiki/data/timeline/), [Awards](https://joedef.github.io/sopranos-wiki/data/awards/), [Ratings](https://joedef.github.io/sopranos-wiki/data/ratings/), [Quotes](https://joedef.github.io/sopranos-wiki/data/quotes/)
- [Glossary](https://joedef.github.io/sopranos-wiki/reference/glossary/): gabagool, capo, omertà, and the dialect
- [Real-World Mafia](https://joedef.github.io/sopranos-wiki/reference/real-world-mafia/): the DeCavalcante family, documented vs. inferred vs. disputed
- [Sources](https://joedef.github.io/sopranos-wiki/reference/sources/)
